NKJV.1: Now, brethren, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our gathering together to Him, we ask you,
NKJV.2: not to be soon shaken in mind or troubled, either by spirit or by word or by letter, as if from us, as though the day of Christ had come.
NKJV.3: Let no one deceive you by any means; for that Day will not come unless the falling away comes first, and the man of sin is revealed, the son of perdition,
NKJV.4: who opposes and exalts himself above all that is called God or that is worshiped, so that he sits as God in the temple of God, showing himself that he is God.
NKJV.5: Do you not remember that when I was still with you I told you these things?
NKJV.6: And now you know what is restraining, that he may be revealed in his own time.
NKJV.7: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only He who now restrains will do so until He is taken out of the way.
NKJV.8: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord will consume with the breath of His mouth and destroy with the brightness of His coming.
NKJV.9: The coming of the lawless one is according to the working of Satan, with all power, signs, and lying wonders,
NKJV.10: and with all unrighteous deception among those who perish, because they did not receive the love of the truth, that they might be saved.
NKJV.11: And for this reason God will send them strong delusion, that they should believe the lie,
NKJV.12: that they all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
NKJV.13: But we are bound to give thanks to God always for you, brethren beloved by the Lord, because God from the beginning chose you for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth,
NKJV.14: to which He called you by our gospel, for the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.15: Therefore, brethren, stand fast and hold the traditions which you were taught, whether by word or our epistle.
NKJV.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself, and our God and Father, who has loved us and given us everlasting consolation and good hope by grace,
NKJV.17: comfort your hearts and establish you in every good word and work.

ESV.1: Now concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him, we ask you, brothers,
ESV.2: not to be quickly shaken in mind or alarmed, either by a spirit or a spoken word, or a letter seeming to be from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord has come.
ESV.3: Let no one deceive you in any way. For that day will not come, unless the rebellion comes first, and the man of lawlessness  is revealed, the son of destruction,
ESV.4: who opposes and exalts himself against every so-called god or object of worship, so that he takes his seat in the temple of God, proclaiming himself to be God.
ESV.5: Do you not remember that when I was still with you I told you these things?
ESV.6: And you know what is restraining him now so that he may be revealed in his time.
ESV.7: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work. Only he who now restrains it will do so until he is out of the way.
ESV.8: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will kill with the breath of his mouth and bring to nothing by the appearance of his coming.
ESV.9: The coming of the lawless one is by the activity of Satan with all power and false signs and wonders,
ESV.10: and with all wicked deception for those who are perishing, because they refused to love the truth and so be saved.
ESV.11: Therefore God sends them a strong delusion, so that they may believe what is false,
ESV.12: in order that all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
ESV.13: But we ought always to give thanks to God for you, brothers beloved by the Lord, because God chose you as the firstfruits  to be saved, through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth.
ESV.14: To this he called you through our gospel, so that you may obtain the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.15: So then, brothers, stand firm and hold to the traditions that you were taught by us, either by our spoken word or by our letter.
ESV.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave us eternal comfort and good hope through grace,
ESV.17: comfort your hearts and establish them in every good work and word.

NASB.1: Now we request you, brethren, with regard to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our gathering together to Him,
NASB.2: that you not be quickly shaken from your composure or be disturbed either by a spirit or a message or a letter as if from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord has come.
NASB.3: Let no one in any way deceive you, for [it will not come] unless the apostasy comes first, and the man of lawlessness is revealed, the son of destruction,
NASB.4: who opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship, so that he takes his seat in the temple of God, displaying himself as being God.
NASB.5: Do you not remember that while I was still with you, I was telling you these things?
NASB.6: And you know what restrains him now, so that in his time he will be revealed.
NASB.7: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only he who now restrains [will do so] until he is taken out of the way.
NASB.8: Then that lawless one will be revealed whom the Lord will slay with the breath of His mouth and bring to an end by the appearance of His coming;
NASB.9: [that is], the one whose coming is in accord with the activity of Satan, with all power and signs and false wonders,
NASB.10: and with all the deception of wickedness for those who perish, because they did not receive the love of the truth so as to be saved.
NASB.11: For this reason God will send upon them a deluding influence so that they will believe what is false,
NASB.12: in order that they all may be judged who did not believe the truth, but took pleasure in wickedness.
NASB.13: But we should always give thanks to God for you, brethren beloved by the Lord, because God has chosen you from the beginning for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and faith in the truth.
NASB.14: It was for this He called you through our gospel, that you may gain the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.15: So then, brethren, stand firm and hold to the traditions which you were taught, whether by word [of mouth] or by letter from us.
NASB.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself and God our Father, who has loved us and given us eternal comfort and good hope by grace,
NASB.17: comfort and strengthen your hearts in every good work and word.

NLT.1: Now, dear brothers and sisters, let us clarify some things about the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and how we will be gathered to meet him.
NLT.2: Don’t be so easily shaken or alarmed by those who say that the day of the Lord has already begun. Don’t believe them, even if they claim to have had a spiritual vision, a revelation, or a letter supposedly from us.
NLT.3: Don’t be fooled by what they say. For that day will not come until there is a great rebellion against God and the man of lawlessness is revealed — the one who brings destruction.
NLT.4: He will exalt himself and defy everything that people call god and every object of worship. He will even sit in the temple of God, claiming that he himself is God.
NLT.5: Don’t you remember that I told you about all this when I was with you?
NLT.6: And you know what is holding him back, for he can be revealed only when his time comes.
NLT.7: For this lawlessness is already at work secretly, and it will remain secret until the one who is holding it back steps out of the way.
NLT.8: Then the man of lawlessness will be revealed, but the Lord Jesus will slay him with the breath of his mouth and destroy him by the splendor of his coming.
NLT.9: This man will come to do the work of Satan with counterfeit power and signs and miracles.
NLT.10: He will use every kind of evil deception to fool those on their way to destruction, because they refuse to love and accept the truth that would save them.
NLT.11: So God will cause them to be greatly deceived, and they will believe these lies.
NLT.12: Then they will be condemned for enjoying evil rather than believing the truth.
NLT.13: As for us, we can’t help but thank God for you, dear brothers and sisters loved by the Lord. We are always thankful that God chose you to be among the first to experience salvation — a salvation that came through the Spirit who makes you holy and through your belief in the truth.
NLT.14: He called you to salvation when we told you the Good News; now you can share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NLT.15: With all these things in mind, dear brothers and sisters, stand firm and keep a strong grip on the teaching we passed on to you both in person and by letter.
NLT.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who loved us and by his grace gave us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope,
NLT.17: comfort you and strengthen you in every good thing you do and say.

WEB.1: Now, brothers, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and our gathering together to him, we ask you
WEB.2: not to be quickly shaken in your mind, nor yet be troubled, either by spirit, or by word, or by letter as from us, saying that the day of Christ had come.
WEB.3: Let no one deceive you in any way. For it will not be, unless the departure comes first, and the man of sin is revealed, the son of destruction,
WEB.4: he who opposes and exalts himself against all that is called God or that is worshiped; so that he sits as God in the temple of God, setting himself up as God.
WEB.5: Don’t you remember that, when I was still with you, I told you these things?
WEB.6: Now you know what is restraining him, to the end that he may be revealed in his own season.
WEB.7: For the mystery of lawlessness already works. Only there is one who restrains now, until he is taken out of the way.
WEB.8: Then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord will kill with the breath of his mouth, and destroy by the manifestation of his coming;
WEB.9: even he whose coming is according to the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders,
WEB.10: and with all deception of wickedness for those who are being lost, because they didn’t receive the love of the truth, that they might be saved.
WEB.11: Because of this, God sends them a working of error, that they should believe a lie;
WEB.12: that they all might be judged who didn’t believe the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
WEB.13: But we are bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers loved by the Lord, because God chose you from the beginning for salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief in the truth;
WEB.14: to which he called you through our Good News, for the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.15: So then, brothers, stand firm, and hold the traditions which you were taught by us, whether by word, or by letter.
WEB.16: Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave us eternal comfort and good hope through grace,
WEB.17: comfort your hearts and establish you in every good work and word.

CSB17.1: Now concerning the coming <sup>ⓗ</sup> of our Lord <sup>ⓘ</sup> Jesus Christ and our being gathered to him: We ask you, brothers and sisters,
CSB17.2: not to be easily upset or troubled, either by a prophecy <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> or by a message or by a letter supposedly from us, alleging that the day of the Lord <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup> has come. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.3: Don’t let anyone deceive you in any way. For that day will not come unless the apostasy <sup>[3]</sup> comes first and the man of lawlessness <sup>[4]</sup> is revealed, the man doomed to destruction.
CSB17.4: He opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god <sup>ⓜ</sup> or object of worship, <sup>ⓝ</sup> so that he sits <sup>[5]</sup> in God’s temple, proclaiming that he himself is God.
CSB17.5: Don’t you remember that when I was still with you I used to tell you about this?
CSB17.6: And you know what currently restrains him, so that he will be revealed in his time. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.7: For the mystery of lawlessness <sup>ⓑ</sup> is already at work, but the one now restraining will do so until he is out of the way,
CSB17.8: and then the lawless one will be revealed. The Lord Jesus will destroy him with the breath of his mouth <sup>ⓒ</sup> and will bring him to nothing at the appearance of his coming.
CSB17.9: The coming of the lawless one is based on Satan’s working, with all kinds of false miracles, signs, and wonders, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.10: and with every wicked deception among those who are perishing. They perish because they did not accept the love of the truth and so be saved. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.11: For this reason God sends them a strong delusion so that they will believe the lie,
CSB17.12: so that all will be condemned <sup>ⓕ</sup>  — those who did not believe <sup>ⓖ</sup> the truth but delighted in unrighteousness.
CSB17.13: But we ought to thank God always for you, brothers and sisters loved by the Lord, because from the beginning <sup>[6]</sup> God has chosen <sup>ⓗ</sup> you for salvation through sanctification <sup>ⓘ</sup> by the Spirit and through belief in the truth.
CSB17.14: He called you to this through our gospel, so that you might obtain the glory <sup>ⓙ</sup> of our Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.15: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm <sup>ⓚ</sup> and hold to the traditions you were taught, <sup>ⓛ</sup> whether by what we said or what we wrote.
CSB17.16: May our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who has loved <sup>ⓜ</sup> us and given us eternal encouragement and good hope by grace,
CSB17.17: encourage your hearts and strengthen you in every good work and word.

AMP.1: Now in regard to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our gathering together to meet Him, we ask you, brothers and sisters,
AMP.2: not to be quickly unsettled or alarmed either by a [so-called prophetic revelation of a] spirit or a message or a letter [alleged to be] from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord has [already] come.
AMP.3: Let no one in any way deceive or entrap you, for that day will not come unless the apostasy comes first [that is, the great rebellion, the abandonment of the faith by professed Christians], and the man of lawlessness is revealed, the son of destruction [the Antichrist, the one who is destined to be destroyed],
AMP.4: who opposes and exalts himself [so proudly and so insolently] above every so-called god or object of worship, so that he [actually enters and] takes his seat in the temple of God, publicly proclaiming that he himself is God.
AMP.5: Do you not remember that when I was still with you, I was telling you these things?
AMP.6: And you know what restrains him now [from being revealed]; it is so that he will be revealed at his own [appointed] time.
AMP.7: For the mystery of lawlessness [rebellion against divine authority and the coming reign of lawlessness] is already at work; [but it is restrained] only until he who now restrains it is taken out of the way.
AMP.8: Then the lawless one [the Antichrist] will be revealed and the Lord Jesus will slay him with the breath of His mouth and bring him to an end by the appearance of His coming.
AMP.9: The coming of the [Antichrist, the lawless] one is through the activity of Satan, [attended] with great power [all kinds of counterfeit miracles] and [deceptive] signs and false wonders [all of them lies],
AMP.10: and by unlimited seduction to evil and with all the deception of wickedness for those who are perishing, because they did not welcome the love of the truth [of the gospel] so as to be saved [they were spiritually blind, and rejected the truth that would have saved them].
AMP.11: Because of this God will send upon them a misleading influence, [an activity of error and deception] so they will believe the lie,
AMP.12: in order that all may be judged and condemned who did not believe the truth [about their sin, and the need for salvation through Christ], but instead took pleasure in unrighteousness.
AMP.13: But we should and are [morally] obligated [as debtors] always to give thanks to God for you, believers beloved by the Lord, because God has chosen you from the beginning for salvation through the sanctifying work of the Spirit [that sets you apart for God's purpose] and by your faith in the truth [of God's word that leads you to spiritual maturity].
AMP.14: It was to this end that He called you through our gospel [the good news of Jesus' death, burial, and resurrection], so that you may obtain and share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.15: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold [tightly] to the traditions which you were taught, whether by word of mouth or by letter from us.
AMP.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself and God our Father, who has loved us and given us everlasting comfort and encouragement and the good [well-founded] hope [of salvation] by His grace,
AMP.17: comfort and encourage and strengthen your hearts [keeping them steadfast and on course] in every good work and word.

MSG.1: Now, friends, read these next words carefully. Slow down and don't go jumping to conclusions regarding the day when our Master, Jesus Christ, will come back and we assemble to welcome him.
MSG.2: Don't let anyone shake you up or get you excited over some breathless report or rumored letter from me that the day of the Master's arrival has come and gone.
MSG.3: Don't fall for any line like that. Before that day comes, a couple of things have to happen. First, the Apostasy. Second, the debut of the Anarchist, a real dog of Satan.
MSG.4: He'll defy and then take over every so-called god or altar. Having cleared away the opposition, he'll then set himself up in God's Temple as "God Almighty."
MSG.5: Don't you remember me going over all this in detail when I was with you? Are your memories that short?
MSG.6: You'll also remember that I told you the Anarchist is being held back until just the right time.
MSG.7: That doesn't mean that the spirit of anarchy is not now at work. It is, secretly and underground.
MSG.8: But the time will come when the Anarchist will no longer be held back, but will be let loose. But don't worry. The Master Jesus will be right on his heels and blow him away. The Master appears and—puff!—the Anarchist is out of there.
MSG.9: The Anarchist's coming is all Satan's work. All his power and signs and miracles are fake,
MSG.10: evil sleight of hand that plays to the gallery of those who hate the truth that could save them.
MSG.11: And since they're so obsessed with evil, God rubs their noses in it—gives them what they want.
MSG.12: Since they refuse to trust truth, they're banished to their chosen world of lies and illusions.
MSG.13: Meanwhile, we've got our hands full continually thanking God for you, our good friends—so loved by God! God picked you out as his from the very start. Think of it: included in God's original plan of salvation by the bond of faith in the living truth.
MSG.14: This is the life of the Spirit he invited you to through the Message we delivered, in which you get in on the glory of our Master, Jesus Christ.
MSG.15: So, friends, take a firm stand, feet on the ground and head high. Keep a tight grip on what you were taught, whether in personal conversation or by our letter.
MSG.16: May Jesus himself and God our Father, who reached out in love and surprised you with gifts of unending help and confidence,
MSG.17: put a fresh heart in you, invigorate your work, enliven your speech.

NIV.1:
The Man of Lawlessness
Concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered to him, we ask you, brothers,
NIV.2: not to become easily unsettled or alarmed by some prophecy, report or letter supposed to have come from us, saying that the day of the Lord has already come.
NIV.3: Don’t let anyone deceive you in any way, for that day will not come until the rebellion occurs and the man of lawlessness is revealed, the man doomed to destruction.
NIV.4: He will oppose and will exalt himself over everything that is called God or is worshiped, so that he sets himself up in God’s temple, proclaiming himself to be God.
NIV.5: Don’t you remember that when I was with you I used to tell you these things?
NIV.6: And now you know what is holding him back, so that he may be revealed at the proper time.
NIV.7: For the secret power of lawlessness is already at work; but the one who now holds it back will continue to do so till he is taken out of the way.
NIV.8: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will overthrow with the breath of his mouth and destroy by the splendor of his coming.
NIV.9: The coming of the lawless one will be in accordance with the work of Satan displayed in all kinds of counterfeit miracles, signs and wonders,
NIV.10: and in every sort of evil that deceives those who are perishing. They perish because they refused to love the truth and so be saved.
NIV.11: For this reason God sends them a powerful delusion so that they will believe the lie
NIV.12: and so that all will be condemned who have not believed the truth but have delighted in wickedness.
NIV.13:
Stand Firm
But we ought always to thank God for you, brothers loved by the Lord, because from the beginning God chose you to be saved through the sanctifying work of the Spirit and through belief in the truth.
NIV.14: He called you to this through our gospel, that you might share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.15: So then, brothers, stand firm and hold to the teachings we passed on to you, whether by word of mouth or by letter.
NIV.16: May our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who loved us and by his grace gave us eternal encouragement and good hope,
NIV.17: encourage your hearts and strengthen you in every good deed and word.

NRSVCE.1: As to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him, we beg you, brothers and sisters,
NRSVCE.2: not to be quickly shaken in mind or alarmed, either by spirit or by word or by letter, as though from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord is already here.
NRSVCE.3: Let no one deceive you in any way; for that day will not come unless the rebellion comes first and the lawless one is revealed, the one destined for destruction.
NRSVCE.4: He opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship, so that he takes his seat in the temple of God, declaring himself to be God.
NRSVCE.5: Do you not remember that I told you these things when I was still with you?
NRSVCE.6: And you know what is now restraining him, so that he may be revealed when his time comes.
NRSVCE.7: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work, but only until the one who now restrains it is removed.
NRSVCE.8: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will destroy with the breath of his mouth, annihilating him by the manifestation of his coming.
NRSVCE.9: The coming of the lawless one is apparent in the working of Satan, who uses all power, signs, lying wonders,
NRSVCE.10: and every kind of wicked deception for those who are perishing, because they refused to love the truth and so be saved.
NRSVCE.11: For this reason God sends them a powerful delusion, leading them to believe what is false,
NRSVCE.12: so that all who have not believed the truth but took pleasure in unrighteousness will be condemned.
NRSVCE.13: But we must always give thanks to God for you, brothers and sisters beloved by the Lord, because God chose you as the first fruits for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and through belief in the truth.
NRSVCE.14: For this purpose he called you through our proclamation of the good news, so that you may obtain the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.15: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold fast to the traditions that you were taught by us, either by word of mouth or by our letter.
NRSVCE.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who loved us and through grace gave us eternal comfort and good hope,
NRSVCE.17: comfort your hearts and strengthen them in every good work and word.

NET.1: Now regarding the arrival of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered to be with him, we ask you, brothers and sisters,
NET.2: not to be easily shaken from your composure or disturbed by any kind of spirit or message or letter allegedly from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord is already here.
NET.3: Let no one deceive you in any way. For that day will not arrive until the rebellion comes and the man of lawlessness is revealed, the son of destruction.
NET.4: He opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship, and as a result he takes his seat in God’s temple, displaying himself as God.
NET.5: Surely you recall that I used to tell you these things while I was still with you.
NET.6: And so you know what holds him back, so that he will be revealed in his own time.
NET.7: For the hidden power of lawlessness is already at work. However, the one who holds him back will do so until he is taken out of the way,
NET.8: and then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord will destroy by the breath of his mouth and wipe out by the manifestation of his arrival.
NET.9: The arrival of the lawless one will be by Satan’s working with all kinds of miracles and signs and false wonders,
NET.10: and with every kind of evil deception directed against those who are perishing, because they found no place in their hearts for the truth so as to be saved.
NET.11: Consequently God sends on them a deluding influence so that they will believe what is false.
NET.12: And so all of them who have not believed the truth but have delighted in evil will be condemned.
NET.13: But we ought to thank God always for you, brothers and sisters loved by the Lord, because God chose you from the beginning for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and faith in the truth.
NET.14: He called you to this salvation through our gospel, so that you may possess the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.15: Therefore, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold on to the traditions that we taught you, whether by speech or by letter.
NET.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who loved us and by grace gave us eternal comfort and good hope,
NET.17: encourage your hearts and strengthen you in every good thing you do or say.
